title: Quantum Computing Basics
content: Quantum computing uses qubits to perform calculations. Unlike classical bits, qubits can exist in superposition.

title: Superposition and Entanglement
content: Superposition allows qubits to be in multiple states simultaneously. Entanglement links qubits so the state of one affects the other.

title: LLM Architecture
content: Large Language Models (LLMs) use the Transformer architecture. Key components include self-attention mechanisms and multi-head attention.

title: RAG Techniques
content: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhances LLMs by retrieving relevant documents from an external knowledge base before generating a response.
